meta.title: Serverless Bot
meta.description: Next.js multi-platform bot console and webhook framework

Api.unauthorized: Not signed in or session expired
Api.botInaccessible: Bot not found or you do not have access

sandbox.INVALID_JSON: Request body is not valid JSON
sandbox.INVALID_BODY: Request body must be a JSON object
sandbox.UNSUPPORTED_BODY: Use schemaVersion: 1 and message.content; top-level text is no longer supported
sandbox.UNSUPPORTED_SCHEMA: schemaVersion does not match the supported wire protocol

InstallStatusHints.localSqlite: Self-hosted / Docker: Node 22.5+, set DATABASE_ENGINE=nodejs-sqlite and SQLITE_PATH (e.g. file:./data.db)
InstallStatusHints.serverless: Vercel: Turso uses TURSO_*; KV uses KV_REST_API_URL / KV_REST_API_TOKEN (read-write). LIBSQL_* and UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_* remain supported as fallbacks

Sponsor.footerNote: If this project helps you, consider sponsoring to keep it maintained.
Sponsor.openInNewTabAria: Open sponsor page in a new tab
Sponsor.hubThanks: Thanks for using Serverless Bot. If you find it useful, sponsorship helps ongoing development.

Ui.confirmDeleteSkill: Delete this skill? Linked Agents will be updated automatically.
Ui.confirmDeleteTool: Delete this tool? Linked Agents will be updated automatically.
Ui.confirmDeleteVendor: Delete this vendor connection? Its models will be removed and Agents using them must be reconfigured.
Ui.confirmDeleteMcp: Delete this MCP server? Linked Agents will be unlinked; save Agents again if needed.
Ui.confirmDeleteFlowNamed: Delete routing “{name}”? This cannot be undone.
Ui.confirmDeleteTriggerNamed: Delete trigger “{name}”? This cannot be undone.
Ui.bindPipelineRequired: Select at least one step pipeline.
Ui.haltLowerPriorityTitle: Stop other routings for this event after match
Ui.haltLowerPriorityDescription: When on, once this routing matches, no other pending routings for the same event will run (engine uses ascending priority; lower numbers first). Site-wide “first match wins” via FLOW_STOP_AFTER_FIRST_MATCH=1.
Ui.executionTargetHint: By default pipelines run in order. With Agent, only that Agent is called; the user message is filled from the event (you can also use an LLM Agent step inside a pipeline).
Ui.noAgentsForFlowHint: No Agents yet. Create one under LLM → Agents and ensure your account has agents:read.

UnauthorizedPage.title: Access denied
UnauthorizedPage.description: You do not have permission to view this page. Ask an administrator for access.

DashboardPage.title: Dashboard
DashboardPage.subtitle: Roll-up of bots, flows, and automation you own—jump into each module from the cards. Charts and run-level reports can grow here over time.
DashboardPage.healthDesc: Share of resources switched on—spot configs that are still off.

serverOverview.title: Server overview
serverOverview.desc: Process & data layer (no secrets). Memory is sampled when this page renders; Serverless may use a different instance per request.
serverOverview.memoryCompact: Heap {heap} MB · RSS {rss} MB · Ext {ext} MB

HomePage.title: Serverless Bot
HomePage.heroTitle: Turn chat platforms into orchestrated automations
HomePage.heroSubtitle: One dashboard for Telegram, Discord, QQ, OneBot 11, Satori, Milky, WeChat Official Accounts, and more—triggers, flows, schedules, and LLM agents in one pipeline. Built for teams that want speed without giving up control.
HomePage.heroHighlight: Create an account in minutes and try message-driven flows in chat before you wire a real bot.
HomePage.heroTrust: Signing up lets you persist configs, collaborate with RBAC, and move from testing to production webhooks when you're ready.
HomePage.capabilitiesDesc: From inbound events to HTTP calls, agent turns, and cron—use modules alone or chain them into one automation story.
HomePage.cardAdapterDesc: Official bot APIs plus OneBot 11, Satori, Milky, and more—credentials and webhooks in one place.
HomePage.cardFlowDesc: Compose steps with branches, HTTP actions, outbound messages, and more.
HomePage.cardTriggerDesc: Match keywords, sources, and finer rules to route events into the right pipeline.
HomePage.cardAgentDesc: Wire vendors and tools so models run in a governed, observable loop.
HomePage.cardScheduleDesc: Run flows on a cadence—digests, reminders, and batch jobs.
HomePage.cardSandboxDesc: Send messages like a normal chat to drive triggers and flows—great for quick iteration.
HomePage.cardDocsDesc: Webhook shapes, schemas, and troubleshooting guides.
HomePage.stepsSubtitle: After you sign up, follow the console in order—the first automation clicks together quickly.
HomePage.step1Desc: Create your account, paste bot credentials in adapters, or start in Chat with test messages.
HomePage.step2Desc: Define what should fire, link flows to agents or step chains, and iterate with traces.
HomePage.step3Desc: Use RBAC for your team, point webhooks to production, and keep improving safely.
HomePage.quickStartDesc: Events arrive via HTTP POST to a stable route; adapters verify and normalize payloads.
HomePage.ctaBandTitle: Sign up today, run your first flow today
HomePage.ctaBandSubtitle: Self-service sign-up unlocks saved bots and flows; GitHub or Passkeys can complement passwords when your admin enables them.
HomePage.footerTagline: Serverless Bot — multi-platform bot console & event orchestration

InstallWizard.intro: For <first>first-time deployment</first>: set database and auth env vars, then run the one-click database install. If the instance is <second>already in use</second> and you only need migrations after an upgrade, open the <upgrade>/upgrade</upgrade> page. On Serverless you can sync env vars to Vercel, redeploy, then finish install.
InstallWizard.phaseNoDb: No database connection. Prefer TURSO_DATABASE_URL + TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN (Vercel Turso), or LIBSQL_*, or SQLITE_PATH for node:sqlite — redeploy, then reopen this page.
InstallWizard.phaseNeedsInstall: Database is connected; you can run the first migration and RBAC bootstrap.
InstallWizard.phaseNeedsUpgrade: Only incremental migrations are needed; redirecting to upgrade. If not, open /upgrade manually.
InstallWizard.installSecretPlaceholder: Must match server env INSTALL_SECRET in production
InstallWizard.installSecretHint: In production set <c1>INSTALL_SECRET</c1> on the platform and enter the same value here so one-click install and Vercel sync work. Dev may omit it; if set, you must fill it. The installed-state cookie is signed with <c2>NEXTAUTH_SECRET</c2> (or <c3>INSTALL_COOKIE_SECRET</c3>); use strong random values in production.
InstallWizard.dbLibsql: Turso / libSQL (Vercel: TURSO_*; LIBSQL_* still supported)
InstallWizard.sqliteHint: Requires Node.js ≥ 22.5. Restart the process after saving, then return here.
InstallWizard.placeholderNextAuthUrl: NEXTAUTH_URL (public root URL, no trailing slash)
InstallWizard.vercelHint: Create a Vercel Personal Access Token and enter the project ID (from project settings). Redeploy after a successful push so vars reach runtime. For Cloudflare Pages/Workers, paste the block above manually (no built-in API).
InstallWizard.vercelSkipDb: Database is already provided via env (e.g. TURSO_*). Sync will not push DB URL/token vars so Turso integration stays intact.
InstallWizard.dbInjectedTursoBody: This process already has TURSO_DATABASE_URL (and token) from e.g. the Vercel Turso integration. You do not need to enter DB credentials here or re-push them via sync below.
InstallWizard.dbInjectedLibsqlBody: LIBSQL_URL is already set. No need to duplicate it here; Vercel sync will not write LIBSQL_* either, so existing project vars stay intact.
InstallWizard.dbInjectedMissingToken: Database URL env is set but the token is missing or invalid. Add TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN or LIBSQL_AUTH_TOKEN for that database in Vercel, then redeploy.

UpgradeWizard.title: Database upgrade
UpgradeWizard.description: First install is done; pending migrations from the repo remain. Run this during a maintenance window (same endpoint as one-click install — applies incremental SQL and fixes RBAC).
UpgradeWizard.migrationUnknown: (Could not read — check primary DB connection.)
UpgradeWizard.installSecretHint: Same as <path>/install</path>: set <c1>INSTALL_SECRET</c1> in production and match header <c2>x-install-secret</c2>.

SandboxChat.emptyThread: No messages yet. Send one to run your message triggers and flows—they’ll answer here.
SandboxChat.noOutbound: No reply was sent for this message.
SandboxChat.title: Chat

SignInPage.title: Sign in
SignInPage.subtitle: Sign in with GitHub to access the console

SignUpPage.title: Sign up
SignUpPage.subtitle: Create an account. You'll be signed in automatically.

AuthSettingsPage.title: Authentication
AuthSettingsPage.description: Control self-service sign-up, third-party sign-in (GitHub, Google, GitLab, Passkeys), and email (SMTP, templates).
AuthSettingsPage.globalDesc: Affects sign-in and self-service sign-up for everyone.
AuthSettingsPage.oauthProvidersDesc: OAuth credentials live in auth_settings. Configure each provider in its tab; adding more tabs later won’t lengthen this page.
AuthSettingsPage.githubDesc: OAuth app credentials are stored in the database (auth_settings). Enter the GitHub Client ID and Secret below. Callback URL must match NEXTAUTH_URL (e.g. https://your.domain/api/auth/callback/github).
AuthSettingsPage.passkeyDesc: RP ID defaults to the hostname of NEXTAUTH_URL; use HTTPS and a real domain in production.
AuthSettingsPage.allowSignup: Allow new accounts via this method
AuthSettingsPage.githubClientIdPlaceholder: Client ID from the GitHub OAuth App
AuthSettingsPage.githubClientSecretPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep the saved secret
AuthSettingsPage.githubClientSecretHint: Secrets are masked after save; only a new value updates the secret.
AuthSettingsPage.googleDesc: Create OAuth 2.0 credentials in Google Cloud Console (APIs & Services → Credentials). Authorized redirect URI must be https://your.domain/api/auth/callback/google (use http://localhost:3001/api/auth/callback/google in dev if that is your app port). Client ID and Secret are stored in auth_settings.
AuthSettingsPage.googleClientSecretPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep the saved secret
AuthSettingsPage.googleClientSecretHint: Secrets are masked after save; only a new value updates the secret.
AuthSettingsPage.gitlabDesc: OAuth application credentials are stored in auth_settings. Enter the Application ID and Secret from your GitLab OAuth app. Callback URL must be https://your.domain/api/auth/callback/gitlab. For self-managed GitLab, set the instance URL below (e.g. https://gitlab.example.com).
AuthSettingsPage.gitlabBaseUrlHint: Leave empty to use GitLab.com. No trailing slash.
AuthSettingsPage.gitlabClientSecretPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep the saved secret
AuthSettingsPage.gitlabClientSecretHint: Secrets are masked after save; only a new value updates the secret.
AuthSettingsPage.emailDesc: For verification, password reset, etc. Enable only after SMTP is configured. Templates support named placeholders: code, link, name—wrap each name in paired curly braces (double-brace style).
AuthSettingsPage.smtpPasswordPlaceholder: Leave empty to keep the saved password
AuthSettingsPage.smtpPasswordHint: After save, passwords are masked; only a new value updates the secret.
AuthSettingsPage.templateHint_verification: Include a code or link placeholder in subject/body; use the variable names code or link inside double curly braces.
AuthSettingsPage.templateHint_passwordReset: Include code or link placeholders; wrap the names code and link in double curly braces.
AuthSettingsPage.templateHint_generic: Test email uses this template; HTML or plain text.
AuthSettingsPage.testEmailHint: Uses the Generic template; if empty, a default test body is sent. Requires email enabled and valid SMTP.

PlatformSettingsPage.title: Platform settings
PlatformSettingsPage.description: Flow/webhook queues, step timeouts, session refresh, and related runtime tuning. Worker/Cron secrets stay in the env whitelist.
PlatformSettingsPage.flowDesc: Per-event budget and whether to stop after the first matching flow.
PlatformSettingsPage.flowStopAfterFirstMatch: Stop remaining flows after any match
PlatformSettingsPage.webhookDesc: KV queue, dedupe, and worker batching. Async flow still requires FLOW_WORKER_SECRET.
PlatformSettingsPage.webhookFlowDedupeOnSuccessOnly: Write dedupe key only after successful flow
PlatformSettingsPage.stepsDesc: call_api default/max timeout; LLM agent tool rounds default (overridable per agent).
PlatformSettingsPage.llmAgentMaxToolRounds: LLM agent max tool rounds (global default)
PlatformSettingsPage.sessionDesc: How often JWT user data is refreshed; hybrid chat SQL strictness.
PlatformSettingsPage.chatSqlRequired: Hybrid chat: forbid KV write if SQL fails
PlatformSettingsPage.linkToAuth: → Authentication (sign-up, SMTP, GitHub, Google, GitLab, Passkeys)

SignInUpForm.noDatabase: Database is not ready. Complete the install wizard first.
SignInUpForm.errSignUpDisabled: Self-service sign-up is disabled by an administrator
SignInUpForm.errEmailTaken: This email is already in use
SignInUpForm.oauthFooterHint: Third-party sign-in above may create an account on first use when your administrator allows it.

AdapterSetupGuide.title: 🚀 Quick start
AdapterSetupGuide.description: Get credentials from the platform and wire them into bots below.
AdapterSetupGuide.usageSectionTitle: How to use in this app
AdapterSetupGuide.tabsAriaLabel: Choose a platform to see setup steps
AdapterSetupGuide.fallbackNoGuide: No built-in guide for “{key}”. See your platform docs.

telegram.step1Body: In Telegram, search for @BotFather—Telegram’s official bot management tool.
telegram.step2Body: Send the /newbot command and follow the prompts for display name and username.
telegram.step3Body: BotFather returns your bot token, e.g. 123456:ABC-DEF1234ghIkl-zyx57W2v1u123ew11.
telegram.step4Body: In BotFather, use /setwebhook to set the webhook URL for receiving updates.
telegram.tip: Keep your bot token secret—do not share it publicly.
telegram.usage1: <lead>1. Copy the bot token</lead> from BotFather
telegram.usage4: <lead>4. Paste the token</lead> into the “Bot Token” field
telegram.usage6: <lead>6. Done</lead>—you can now receive Telegram events

discord.getCredentialsTitle: Get Discord Bot Token and Public Key
discord.step1Title: Step 1: Open the Discord Developer Portal
discord.step1Body: Open the <portal>Discord Developer Portal</portal> and sign in with your Discord account.
discord.step2Body: Click “New Application”, enter a name, and create it.
discord.step3Body: Open “Bot” in the sidebar, click “Add Bot”, then under “TOKEN” click “Copy”.
discord.step5Title: Step 5: Set the Interactions Endpoint URL
discord.step5Before: On “General Information”, set “INTERACTIONS ENDPOINT URL” to your server (e.g.
discord.step6Title: Optional: Invite the bot to a server
discord.step6Body: Under “OAuth2” → “URL Generator”, select bot and the scopes you need, then use the URL to invite the bot.
discord.tip: Bot Token and Public Key are sensitive—do not hard-code or leak them.
discord.usage1: <lead>1. Copy Bot Token and Public Key</lead> from the Developer Portal
discord.usage4BotToken: • <field>Bot Token</field>: paste your Discord bot token
discord.usage6: <lead>6. Done</lead>—you can now receive Discord events

qq.getCredentialsTitle: Get QQ bot AppID and AppSecret
qq.step1Title: Step 1: Open the QQ Open Platform
qq.step1Body: Open <portal>QQ Open Platform</portal>, sign in with QQ, and open the bot console.
qq.step2Body: Click “Create bot”, fill in name, avatar, description, etc., and submit for review.
qq.step3Body: After approval, under “Developer settings” find <code1>AppID</code1> and <code2>AppSecret</code2>.
qq.step4Body: Under “Developer settings” → “Callback configuration”, set the message endpoint to:
qq.step5Body: Under “Event subscriptions”, enable the events you need, for example:
qq.tip: AppSecret is used for signature verification—keep it private. After you set the callback, QQ sends a verification request; the app handles it automatically.
qq.warnTitle: QQ bots have message sending limits:
qq.warnPassive: Passive replies: within 5 minutes of a message, up to 5 replies
qq.warnActive: Active pushes: only after the user has triggered interaction
qq.usage1: <lead>1. Copy AppID and AppSecret</lead> from the open platform
qq.usage6: <lead>6. Copy the generated Webhook URL</lead> into the QQ open platform
qq.usage7: <lead>7. Done</lead>—you can now receive QQ events

onebot11.step1Body: Use an implementation that supports OneBot 11 over HTTP (e.g. NapCat, go-cqhttp) and enable its <portal>HTTP API + event post</portal> features.
onebot11.step2Title: Step 2: Point event post to this console
onebot11.step2Body: In your client, set the HTTP webhook / report URL to this app’s webhook (see below). Use JSON POST with standard OneBot 11 event bodies.
onebot11.step3Body: After you create a bot record under this adapter, the webhook path is:
onebot11.step4Body: Optional: set the same custom header X-OneBot-Secret on the client and paste the value into “Webhook secret” in the bot form so only your bridge can post events.
onebot11.tip: HTTP API base URL is where send_private_msg / send_group_msg live—often the same host as your bridge’s HTTP API port.
onebot11.usage1: <lead>1. Create a bot</lead> under this adapter
onebot11.usage2: <lead>2. Fill in</lead> the HTTP API base URL (and token if enabled on the bridge)
onebot11.usageFieldToken: • <field>API Token</field>: must match the bridge Authorization if required
onebot11.usageFieldSecret: • <field>Webhook secret</field>: if set, bridge must send header X-OneBot-Secret with the same value
onebot11.usage4: <lead>4. Copy the webhook URL</lead> into your bridge’s event/report configuration
onebot11.usage5: <lead>5. Save and test</lead>—send a message through the bridge and watch triggers fire

satori.step1Body: Satori is a universal chat protocol. Platform <portal>SDKs / adapters</portal> normalize events; this console acts as an app that receives Webhooks and calls HTTP APIs to send messages.
satori.step2Title: Step 2: Configure Webhook on the bridge
satori.step2Body: In your Satori SDK, add an HTTP webhook URL (JSON POST). Header <lead>Satori-Opcode: 0</lead> means EVENT; the JSON body follows the Event schema in the protocol.
satori.step3Body: After you create a bot under this adapter, point the SDK webhook to:
satori.step4Body: If you set “Webhook secret” here, configure the SDK to send Authorization: Bearer with the same token. Leave empty to skip verification.
satori.tip: Sending messages uses the SDK HTTP API (e.g. message.create). Set the API base URL plus Satori-Platform and Satori-User-ID headers to match the logged-in bot.
satori.usage1: <lead>1. Create a bot</lead> under this adapter
satori.usageFieldUserId: • <field>Satori-User-ID</field>: the bot’s user id on that platform
satori.usageFieldToken: • <field>API Token</field>: Bearer for /v1 RPC when the SDK requires it
satori.usageFieldSecret: • <field>Webhook secret</field>: Bearer the SDK sends to this app (optional)
satori.usage3: <lead>3. Register the webhook URL</lead> in the Satori SDK and save
satori.usage4: <lead>4. Test</lead>—send a message and verify flows run

milky.step1Title: Step 1: Run a Milky protocol server
milky.step1Body: Milky is an HTTP/WebSocket protocol for QQ bots. Run a <portal>Milky server</portal> with HTTP enabled (/api and /event, or WebHook push).
milky.step2Title: Step 2: Point WebHook at this console
milky.step2Body: Configure the server to POST JSON events to your webhook URL. The body matches the WebSocket event shape; <lead>message_receive</lead> means an inbound message.
milky.step3Body: After you create a bot here, set the protocol server WebHook to:
milky.step4Body: If the server attaches access_token to WebHook POSTs, put the same value in “Webhook secret”; requests must include <lead>Authorization: Bearer …</lead>. Use the “access_token” field for outbound /api calls.
milky.tip: Outbound calls use <lead>POST /api/send_private_message</lead> and <lead>send_group_message</lead>. Base URL is the server root (no trailing slash), per the <portal>communication guide</portal>.
milky.usage1: <lead>1. Create a bot</lead> and set the server base URL
milky.usageFieldToken: • <field>access_token</field>: Bearer for /api when the server requires it
milky.usageFieldSecret: • <field>Webhook secret</field>: Bearer the server sends to this app (optional)
milky.usage3: <lead>3. After saving</lead>, trigger a message and check the console
milky.usage4: <lead>4. Wire triggers / flows</lead> for auto-replies (friend, group, temp message_receive)

wechat_mp.setupTitle: WeChat Official Account (server config + customer service messages)
wechat_mp.step1Body: In the <portal>WeChat Official Accounts platform</portal>, open Development → Basic configuration for AppID and AppSecret. Before saving server URL, create a bot here and copy its webhook URL. The Token must match “Server config Token” in the bot form exactly.
wechat_mp.step2Body: Supports <lead>plain</lead>, <lead>secure</lead>, and <lead>compatible</lead> modes: after you pick a mode in MP, paste the generated <lead>EncodingAESKey (43 chars)</lead> into the bot form. If POST carries an Encrypt packet, we AES-decrypt then parse inner XML; in compatible mode a plain packet still uses plain signature checks. Only user <lead>text</lead> (MsgType=text) becomes events; subscribe-style events are not handled. Replies use the <lead>customer service</lead> message API.
wechat_mp.step3Title: Step 3: Server URL (GET verify + POST messages)
wechat_mp.step3Body: Set the server URL in MP “Server configuration” to (HTTPS, ICP-compliant domain as required by WeChat):
wechat_mp.step4Body: Submit runs a GET check: plain mode uses signature; secure/compatible uses <lead>msg_signature</lead> and you decrypt echostr and return the plaintext echo. POST follows the same token rules (cipher uses SHA1 over sorted token, timestamp, nonce, Encrypt).
wechat_mp.tip: access_token is cached via AppID/AppSecret; EncodingAESKey and AppID must match MP in cipher mode. With EncodingAESKey set and platform webhook async OFF, flow steps that text-reply to the current user use one encrypted passive HTTP reply; async mode or other openids still use the customer-service API. Only the last outbound text in a single webhook wins. If nothing arrives, check mode, keys, and subscribe-only traffic (events not handled yet).
wechat_mp.usage1: <lead>1. Create a bot</lead> under this adapter
wechat_mp.usageFieldToken: • <field>Server config Token</field>: same as MP “Server configuration” for signature checks
wechat_mp.usageFieldEncodingAESKey: • <field>EncodingAESKey</field>: 43 chars for secure/compatible; leave empty for plain only
wechat_mp.usage3: <lead>3. Paste the webhook URL</lead> into the MP console and complete verification
wechat_mp.usage4: <lead>4. Test</lead>—send a text in the chat and confirm triggers and flows

AgentFormPage.alertSelectModel: Pick a preset model. If the list is empty, add a vendor profile and models under “Model connections” first.
AgentFormPage.presetHintNew: Choose from vendors and models saved under “Model connections”; auth and Base URL live on the vendor profile.
AgentFormPage.presetHintEdit: Auth and Base URL are maintained on the vendor profile under “Model connections”.
AgentFormPage.emptyModelsNew: No preset models yet. Go to <link>Model connections</link> to add a vendor and models.
AgentFormPage.sysVarsSummaryNew: Built-in variables ($'{'sys.*'}', computed before each LLM call)
AgentFormPage.sysVarsSummaryEdit: Built-in variables ($'{'sys.*'}', computed on each call)
AgentFormPage.promptExtrasNew: Same as flows: <msgVar></msgVar> and <expr></expr>; skill bodies and this step’s user prompt follow the same rules.
AgentFormPage.promptExtrasEdit: Flow variable and expression interpolation matches pipeline steps; skills and this user prompt also support <sys></sys>.

sysVarHelp.nowLocal: Current local date/time (full, using runtime TZ and locale)
sysVarHelp.cwd: Process working directory (often deploy root on serverless)

AgentFormPage.skillsHintNew: Maintain content under LLM → Skills. Injection is controlled by “Skill injection” below: default is summary (name, blurb, id); choose “full” or load by id in an LLM step for full text.
AgentFormPage.skillsHintEdit: Controlled by “Skill injection” below; LLM steps in flows can override or load full text by skill id for the current turn.
AgentFormPage.injectFullNew: Full skill text in system prompt
AgentFormPage.injectNoneNew: None (load full text only when a flow step specifies an id)
AgentFormPage.injectFootnoteNew: Written to extra_json.skillInject; you can also override in “Extra JSON” below (merged on save).
AgentFormPage.injectFootnoteEdit: Synced with skillInject in extra_json; the select value is written there on save.
AgentFormPage.memoryHintNew: Per session (DM peer or group), recent user/assistant text is loaded from the chat store and prepended to the model request. Requires events to resolve a session; if off, only this step’s user prompt is sent.
AgentFormPage.memoryHintEdit: Synced with extra_json memoryMode / memoryWindowTurns; inbound webhook traffic and assistant replies append to the same stream for sliding-window reads.
AgentFormPage.memoryWindowFootnote: One turn = one user + one assistant message. Default 5 turns (~10 lines), max 50. Applies only to sliding window.
AgentFormPage.toolsHintNew: Each row is one OpenAI tool; merged with MCP tools for the model. Tool calls run in the flow; self-hosted tools win over MCP on name clash.
AgentFormPage.toolsHintEdit: Merged with MCP in the request; self-hosted tools take precedence when names collide.
AgentFormPage.mcpHintNew: Streamable HTTP remotes; listTools is merged per request. Configure URL and headers on the MCP page.
AgentFormPage.mcpHintEdit: Configure Streamable HTTP under “MCP”; remote tool definitions are merged at runtime.
AgentFormPage.emptyMcp: No MCP servers yet. Open <link>MCP servers</link>.
AgentFormPage.notFound: Agent not found or access denied

VendorProfilePage.hintProfileExtraJson: Root object may include http: completionsPath (relative or absolute URL), auth (bearer|api_key|authorization_raw), apiKeyHeaderName, headers (values may use {{apiKey}}), body (shallow merge), omitBodyKeys, maxTokensField (e.g. max_completion_tokens).
VendorProfilePage.labelNewApiKey: New API Key (leave empty to keep existing)
VendorProfilePage.presetModelsDescription: Model IDs used in API calls (e.g. gpt-4o-mini). Optional extra_json is merged into the Agent’s extra by default (the Agent can still override).
VendorProfilePage.alertJsonInvalid: Extra fields must be valid JSON
VendorProfilePage.confirmDeleteModel: Delete this preset model? Agents using it will be unlinked.

AdapterConfigForm.enableHint: When enabled, webhook events for this platform are accepted

users.title: Users
users.description: Manage console users and their roles

profile.title: Profile
profile.githubNotLinkedHint: Not linked — use the button to link.
profile.googleNotLinkedHint: Not linked — use the button to link.
profile.gitlabNotLinkedHint: Not linked — use the button to link.

adapter.title: Adapters
adapter.description: Platforms available in this deployment. Open one for setup steps and bot management.
adapter.adapterBotCount: {count, plural, =0 {0 bots} one {# bot} other {# bots}}

botEdit.webhookHint: Set this URL in your platform’s webhook settings.
botEdit.enabledHint: When disabled, events for this bot are ignored.

triggerHub.title: Triggers
triggerHub.description: Define when events fire: event type, match rules, and permissions. Triggers can be reused across multiple routings.

notice.name: Notice triggers
notice.description: Member changes, mutes, and other notices

message.title: Message triggers
message.description: Conditions for DM and group chat messages

request.title: Request triggers
request.description: Conditions for friend requests and group invites

notice.title: Notice triggers
notice.description: Conditions for member changes, mutes, and similar notices

message.title: Message routing
message.description: How to handle DM and group chat messages

request.title: Request routing
request.description: How to handle friend requests and group invites

notice.title: Notice routing
notice.description: How to handle member changes, mutes, and notices

agents.title: LLM Agents
agents.description: Pick a preset model from Model connections, then prompts, skills, and tools. Invoke from pipelines with the LLM Agent step.

llmSkills.title: Skills
llmSkills.description: Skill bodies are merged into the Agent system prompt; enable per agent when editing.

llmTools.title: Tools
llmTools.description: Each entry is one OpenAI tool; implementation steps mirror step pipelines but are scoped to this tool. Selected tools are sent with the model request.

llmVendors.title: Model connections
llmVendors.description: Configure a vendor (shared API key and base URL), then add model names. Agents pick a preset model without re-entering secrets.

llmMcp.title: MCP servers
llmMcp.description: Remote Streamable HTTP MCP endpoints. When enabled on an Agent, tools are listed before each call. Bridge local stdio MCP over HTTP before entering a URL.

schedule.title: Scheduled tasks
schedule.description: Run a step pipeline on a Cron schedule (same step types as event routing). Set CRON_TICK_SECRET and call POST /api/internal/cron/tick on a schedule (header x-cron-tick-secret).
schedule.empty: No tasks yet. Prepare a step pipeline and a bot, then bind a Cron expression here.

job.title: Step pipelines
job.description: Reusable ordered steps invoked from event routing or scheduled tasks.

jobEditor.newSubtitle: Create a pipeline, then add and reorder steps.
jobEditor.enabledHint: When off, event routings that use this pipeline will not run these steps.
jobEditor.agentPickPlaceholder: Pick from list (fills Agent ID below)
jobEditor.noAgentsBefore: No Agents yet. Create one under

llmToolSteps.implementIntro: Same step types as pipelines, with extras for tool chains: parse/stringify JSON, Base64, URL encode/decode. Function arguments from the model are merged into variables. Put text for the model in toolResult (e.g. via Stringify JSON), otherwise the last step’s JSON output is used.
llmToolSteps.emptyHint: No steps yet. Example: Call API → extract data / set variable toolResult.

triggerEditor.namePlaceholderAuto: Leave empty to auto-name from type and time
triggerEditor.adapterSectionHint: By default all platforms and bots; narrow as needed. Bot list comes from instances you created.
triggerEditor.platformAllow: Only selected platforms (check or type below)
triggerEditor.platformDeny: Exclude selected platforms (check or type below)
triggerEditor.botAllow: Only selected bots (check or type IDs below)
triggerEditor.botDeny: Exclude selected bots (check or type IDs below)
triggerEditor.botsEmptyHint: If the list is empty, type IDs manually; when data loads, use checkboxes above.
triggerEditor.manualBotIdsLabel: Manual bot IDs (comma-separated, merged with checks)
triggerEditor.subtypeHint: Empty means no filter; checked subtypes only will match.
triggerEditor.idListPlaceholder: One ID per line or comma-separated
triggerEditor.groupListPlaceholder: One group ID per line or comma-separated

flowHub.title: Event routing
flowHub.subtitle: Bind triggers to targets: hand events to an Agent conversation, or run a step pipeline in order

firstVisit.dismissNever: Don’t show again on this device

llm_assets.description: Skills, Tools, MCP, and how they attach to Agents.

platform_ext.title: Platform extensions
platform_ext.description: Discord commands, QQ, and other platform features.

hub.title: Getting started · Hub
hub.subtitle: Walk through each sidebar area. Completing or skipping a section only updates progress; to enter the main console, click “Finish hub” below.
hub.pendingSome: {count} section(s) not marked done or skipped

errors.needBotDraft: Create a bot under “Bot access” first (if you already did but lost the local draft, create one on the adapters page and try again).
errors.needBotDraftShort: Bot draft required: create a bot under “Bot access” first.
errors.unknownAdapter: This adapter is not available. Refresh and try again.

sectionClient.deniedDescription: Your account cannot open this onboarding section.

overview.cardDescription: Bot access: platform credentials and bot instances. Automation: triggers → event routing → pipelines or Agents. LLM: models and toolchains. System: who can use the console.
overview.hint: Follow the hub cards in order; do real work on each page, then return here to update progress.

bot_access.cardDescription: Configure adapters, then create your bot instance here; use Chat to try messages end-to-end.
bot_access.adapterNotConfiguredHint: This adapter has not been saved on the adapters page yet; you can still create a bot. For platform-level settings, use Open adapters above first.
bot_access.noAdaptersHint: No registered adapters were found. Check that adapter modules are loaded.
bot_access.draftHint: If you only read the docs, you can mark complete below. Clear local draft:

automation.cardDescription: Triggers filter events → routing picks Agent vs pipelines → pipelines are ordered steps; schedules run a pipeline on Cron.
automation.triggerIntro: Triggers decide which messages/notifications to handle.
automation.flowIntro: Routing binds triggers to targets (pipelines or an Agent).
automation.jobIntro: Pipelines are ordered steps, reusable from multiple routes or schedules.
automation.scheduleIntro: Schedules bind Cron to an existing pipeline and bot.

step_ref.cardDescription: Skim step types, then design jobs or toolchains.

llm_runtime.cardDescription: Add vendor profiles and preset models, then create Agents; flows can target an Agent.

llm_assets.cardDescription: Prompt snippets, OpenAI-style tools, and MCP servers.

rbac.cardDescription: Roles bundle permissions; users can have multiple roles.
rbac.createSampleRole: Create sample role and mark done

api.agentPrompt: You are a concise, friendly assistant.

FlowTemplateHint.liStepVars: Prior-step variables: names from fields like “save as variable”; use dot paths, e.g. $'{'userInfo'}', $'{'apiResult.data'}'.
FlowTemplateHint.liEvent: event: the firing event. Common message fields: rawContent, messageId, groupId, sender.userId, sender.nickname. Example: $'{'event.rawContent'}'.
FlowTemplateHint.liMessage: message: alias of event in expressions, e.g. $'{'message.rawContent'}'.
FlowTemplateHint.liFlowJob: flow / job: the current routing and step pipeline (Job) objects.
FlowTemplateHint.liBot: bot: current bot with id, name, platform only.
FlowTemplateHint.liExpr: Expressions: inside braces you may use sandboxed JavaScript beyond simple paths (see runtime).

QQSettings.webhookHint: Set this URL in the QQ Open Platform as the event callback. Allowed ports: 80, 443, 8080, 8443.
QQSettings.intentsIntro: Subscribe to events in the QQ Open Platform. Common intents:
QQSettings.limitDm: DM: 4 proactive messages/month; passive replies valid 60 minutes, up to 5 times
QQSettings.limitGroup: Group: 4 proactive messages/month; passive replies valid 5 minutes, up to 5 times
QQSettings.limitGuild: Guild: 20 proactive messages/day per sub-channel; passive replies valid 5 minutes
QQSettings.limitDeprecated: Proactive messages were discontinued as of 2025-04-21

StepTypes.pageSubtitle: Browse by category; the same groups appear in pipeline and LLM tool step pickers.

descriptions.send_message: Send a message to a user or group
descriptions.set_variable: Set or update a context variable
descriptions.random_reply: Pick one reply from a list
descriptions.template_message: Render a message from a template
descriptions.forward_message: Forward a message to another chat
descriptions.llm_agent: Run a configured Agent; tool calls can run linked implementation steps and return results to the model
descriptions.parse_json: Parse JSON text into an object variable (API or model output)
descriptions.stringify_json: Serialize a variable to JSON (e.g. toolResult for the model)
descriptions.url_encode: encodeURIComponent for query strings and paths
